title: Frontend UX Polish
description: Use when improving an existing UI's readability, hierarchy, accessibility, responsiveness, interaction feedback, or motion without changing product intent.

input_schema.ui_description: string — current layout, user pain points, responsive issues, hierarchy problems (e.g. 'Card-based dashboard; cards stack vertically on mobile but horizontal scroll on 768-1024px tablets; primary metrics buried among secondary cards')
input_schema.constraints: string — WCAG level, brand palette, CSS framework, max animation duration, supported browsers (e.g. 'WCAG AA, Material 3 palette, Tailwind CSS, 200ms max animation, Chrome 90+, iOS 13+')

surface_types.Dashboard/ops tool: Prioritize scan, compare, repeated action, density, filters, status states.
surface_types.Form/settings: Prioritize labels, grouping, validation, focus order, and error placement.
surface_types.Editor/canvas/tool: Prioritize toolbars, selection states, keyboard flow, and stable dimensions.
surface_types.Commerce/content: Prioritize media clarity, comparison, price/spec hierarchy, and trust signals.
surface_types.Game/interactive: Prioritize controls, hit targets, motion feedback, and responsive canvas.
surface_types.Marketing/hero: Prioritize first-viewport signal, media relevance, and conversion path.

instructions: Classify UI surface type and state the primary user task.
Identify the top three information priorities and preserve that order in the proposed layout.
Audit relevant dimensions: hierarchy, layout, typography, color/contrast, interaction, responsiveness, motion, and assets.
Define concrete layout rules including grid/flex behavior, min/max widths, touch targets, overflow handling, sticky/fixed behavior, and mobile/tablet/desktop breakpoints.
Define accessibility requirements including WCAG target, contrast ratios, visible focus, semantic labels, reduced motion, keyboard reachability, and error messaging.
Use motion only for state changes, feedback, or continuity; specify duration/easing and reduced-motion behavior.
Keep visual style consistent with the existing design system, or define a small palette and type scale with token names if no system exists.
Provide verification steps for viewport sizes, contrast checks, keyboard path, screen-reader labels when relevant, and regression risks.
Do not recommend vague cleanup; include measurable values for spacing, font sizes, breakpoints, contrast, animation duration, and hit targets.

examples.input: Card-based dashboard; cards stack on mobile but horizontal scroll on 768-1024px; primary metrics buried. Constraints: WCAG AA, Tailwind, 200ms max animation.
examples.output: Typography: bump heading from 1.25rem to 1.5rem, body stays 1rem (contrast ratio 4.8:1 verified). Spacing: adopt 8px grid, cards get 16px gap. Layout: <=768px single column, 769-1024px 2-col grid (no scroll), >=1025px 3-col. Motion: 150ms fade-in for primary metric cards on mount (state transition), respect prefers-reduced-motion. Hierarchy: primary metrics pinned top row with accent border-left.
